items.title: Regularization and model size
items.description: L1 and L2 penalties on weight vectors control effective model complexity; L1 tends to produce sparse weights, L2 shrinks weights smoothly without forcing exact zeros.

items.title: Nearest-neighbor and clustering
items.description: k-NN, k-means and DBSCAN rely on distances between feature vectors; the chosen metric determines which points group together.

items.title: Semantic retrieval and RAG
items.description: Vector databases rank documents by cosine similarity or L2 distance in embedding space to ground LLM responses in relevant context.

items.title: Training stability
items.description: Gradient norm clipping and weight norm constraints use L2 norms to keep optimization steps bounded during deep learning and LLM fine-tuning.
